name: Tomographic Baggage Surgery
description: Sweep and rotate an X-ray slice through an opaque suitcase, reconstruct the target volume, then guide an extraction probe from orthogonal views without touching innocent solids.
natural_language: Locate and extract the marked contraband without damaging any innocent object. Collect separated X-ray slices across multiple rigid suitcase orientations, lock the surgery frame, register the probe across orthogonal views, capture the target geometrically, and withdraw it cleanly.

metadata.variant_count_kind: 2 mirrors × 4 primitive-order rotations × 3 palettes × 361×501×401 rounded target coordinates
metadata.notes: All slice records derive from rigidly rotated local primitives; surgery begins only after an explicit orientation lock, and the independent grader reconstructs view coordinates and swept carried-target collisions.
